page1.grammarTopic: Past Simple — storytelling with sequence words
page1.grammarExplanation: In storytelling, we use Past Simple with SEQUENCE WORDS to connect events and make stories flow naturally. Sequence words: First (эхлээд), Then (дараа нь), After that (үүний дараа), Next (дараачийн), Suddenly (гэнэт), Finally (эцэст нь), At first (эхэндээ), Later (хожим). These words tell the listener WHEN and IN WHAT ORDER things happened.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: Түүх ярихдаа Past Simple-г ДАРААЛАЛЫН ҮГС-тэй хослуулдаг. Дараалалын үгс: First (эхлээд), Then (дараа нь), After that (үүний дараа), Suddenly (гэнэт), Finally (эцэст нь), At first (эхэндээ), Later (хожим). Эдгээр үгс үйл явдлуудын ДАРААЛАЛыг харуулдаг.
commonMistakes: ❌ First I go to school.  ✅ First I WENT to school.
❌ Then suddenly I am happy.  ✅ Then suddenly I WAS happy.
❌ After that I move.  ✅ After that I MOVED.
❌ Finally I pass.  ✅ Finally I PASSED.

page2.readingPassage: Let me tell you my life story. I was born in Ulaanbaatar in 2008. I grew up in a small apartment near the city centre. At first, life was simple and very happy. When I was six, I started primary school. At first, English was very difficult for me. But then I found a wonderful teacher. After that, I practised English every single day. Suddenly, I realised I could actually speak it! Finally, English became my favourite subject. Now I am telling my story in English — and that makes me incredibly proud!

page1.grammarExplanation: The Present Perfect (have/has + past participle) describes LIFE EXPERIENCES — things you have done at some unspecified time in your life, without saying exactly WHEN. Compare: 'I have been to Seoul.' (experience, no specific time) vs 'I went to Seoul last year.' (specific time = use Past Simple!). Signal words: ever, never, already, yet, just, recently, before, once, twice.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: Present Perfect (have/has + past participle) нь АМЬДРАЛЫН ТУРШЛАГА-г тодорхой цагийг заалгүй илэрхийлдэг. Харьцуул: 'I have been to Seoul.' (туршлага, цаг заагаагүй) VS 'I went to Seoul last year.' (тодорхой цаг = Past Simple хэрэглэ!). Дохио үгс: ever, never, already, yet, just, recently.
commonMistakes: ❌ I have been to Seoul last year.  ✅ I WENT to Seoul last year. (specific time!)
❌ Have you been there yesterday?  ✅ Did you go there yesterday?
❌ I have never went there.  ✅ I have never BEEN there.
❌ Has she went to Japan?  ✅ Has she BEEN to Japan?

page2.readingPassage: Bat loves exploring new places. He has been to many beautiful places across Mongolia. He has visited the Gobi Desert, Lake Khovsgol, and the Orkhon Valley. But he has never been outside Mongolia — not yet! His dream is South Korea. Sarnai has already been abroad — she visited China two years ago. She said it was completely unforgettable. So — have you ever been to another country? If not, where would you go first?

page1.grammarExplanation: Present Perfect uses HAVE/HAS + PAST PARTICIPLE (the 3rd form of the verb). Regular past participles add -ed (studied, visited, cooked). Irregular past participles must be memorized: eat→eaten, see→seen, do→done, go→gone, write→written, speak→spoken, ride→ridden, fly→flown, break→broken, forget→forgotten, win→won. This describes experiences without saying the exact time.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: Present Perfect нь HAVE/HAS + PAST PARTICIPLE (3-р хэлбэр) ашигладаг. Ердийн үйл үгс: -ed нэм (studied, visited). Тэгдэггүй үйл үгс: цээжлэх ёстой. eat→eaten, see→seen, do→done, go→gone, write→written, speak→spoken, ride→ridden, fly→flown, break→broken, forget→forgotten. Яг ХЭЗЭЭ болсныг заалгүй туршлагаа хэлдэг.
commonMistakes: ❌ I have ate sushi.  ✅ I have EATEN sushi.

❌ Have you ever did that?  ✅ Have you ever DONE that?
❌ He has went to Korea.  ✅ He has GONE to Korea.

page1.grammarTopic: Present Perfect with for and since — talking about duration
page1.grammarExplanation: Use FOR with a PERIOD OF TIME (how long): for 3 years, for a month, for ages, for a long time. Use SINCE with a POINT IN TIME (start): since 2010, since Monday, since I was born, since last year. These go with Present Perfect when the situation continues to NOW: 'I have studied English for 30 days.' (started 30 days ago, still true now!)
page1.grammarExplanationMn: FOR нь ХУГАЦААНЫ УРТЫГ (хэр удаан) илэрхийлдэг: for 3 years, for a month, for ages. SINCE нь ЭХЛЭХ ЦЭГийг илэрхийлдэг: since 2010, since Monday, since last year. Эдгээрийг Present Perfect-тэй хэрэглэдэг — нөхцөл байдал ОДОО хүртэл үргэлжилж байгааг харуулна.
commonMistakes: ❌ I study English since a month.  ✅ I have studied FOR a month.
❌ I have studied since 3 months.  ✅ I have studied FOR 3 months.
❌ She lives here since 2010.  ✅ She HAS LIVED here since 2010.
❌ I know him for years.  ✅ I HAVE KNOWN him for years.

page1.grammarTopic: Present Perfect vs Past Simple — when to use each tense
page1.grammarExplanation: KEY RULE: Use PRESENT PERFECT when there is NO specific time (experience, recent events, continuing situations). Use PAST SIMPLE when there IS a specific time (yesterday, last year, in 2020, when I was 6). Signal words for Present Perfect: ever, never, already, yet, just, recently, for, since. Signal words for Past Simple: yesterday, last week, ago, in [year], when.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: ГОЛ ДҮРЭМ: PRESENT PERFECT — тодорхой цаг БАЙХГҮЙ үед (туршлага, саяхан болсон зүйл, үргэлжилж буй нөхцөл байдал). PAST SIMPLE — тодорхой цаг БАЙГАА үед (yesterday, last year, in 2020, when I was 6). PP дохио үгс: ever, never, already, yet, just, recently, for, since. PS дохио үгс: yesterday, last week, ago, in [year].
commonMistakes: ❌ I have been there last year.  ✅ I WENT there last year. (specific!)
❌ Did you ever eat sushi?  ✅ HAVE you ever eaten sushi?
❌ When have you gone?  ✅ WHEN DID you go? (when = Past Simple!)
❌ I lived here since 2010.  ✅ I HAVE LIVED here since 2010.

page2.readingPassage: Today is the big review! Let's compare the two tenses. Bat has been to many places in Mongolia — he went to the Gobi last summer. He has eaten many interesting foods — he tried Korean food two years ago. He has studied English for 35 days — he started at the beginning of this month. See the pattern? When we say WHEN, we use Past Simple. When there is NO specific time, we use Present Perfect. Now you understand both tenses!

page1.grammarTopic: Comparative adjectives — taller, smarter, more beautiful
page1.grammarExplanation: Comparative adjectives compare TWO people or things. SHORT adjectives (1-2 syllables): add -ER + than → tall→taller, smart→smarter, fast→faster. Irregular spelling: big→bigger (double), nice→nicer (-e). LONG adjectives (3+ syllables): use MORE + adjective + than → more beautiful, more intelligent, more expensive. IRREGULAR: good→better, bad→worse, far→further.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: Харьцуулах тэмдэг нэр нь ХОЁР хүн эсвэл зүйлийг харьцуулдаг. БОГИНО тэмдэг нэр (1-2 үе): -ER + than нэм → tall→taller, smart→smarter. УРТ тэмдэг нэр (3+ үе): MORE + тэмдэг нэр + than → more beautiful, more intelligent. ДУР МЭДЭН хэлбэр: good→better, bad→worse, far→further.
commonMistakes: ❌ He is more tall than me.  ✅ He is TALLER than me.
❌ She is beautifuler.  ✅ She is MORE BEAUTIFUL.
❌ He is taller then me.  ✅ He is taller THAN me.
❌ This is more good.  ✅ This is BETTER.

page1.grammarTopic: Superlative adjectives — the tallest, the best, the most beautiful
page1.grammarExplanation: Superlative adjectives compare ONE thing to ALL others in a group. Always use THE before a superlative. SHORT adjectives: THE + adjective + EST → the tallest, the smallest, the biggest. LONG adjectives (3+ syllables): THE MOST + adjective → the most beautiful, the most expensive, the most popular. IRREGULAR: good→the best, bad→the worst, far→the furthest.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: Superlative тэмдэг нэр нь нэг зүйлийг БҮГДТЭЙ харьцуулдаг. Superlative-ийн өмнө ҮРГЭЛЖ THE хэрэглэнэ. БОГИНО тэмдэг нэр: THE + тэмдэг нэр + EST → the tallest, the biggest. УРТ тэмдэг нэр: THE MOST + тэмдэг нэр → the most beautiful. ДУР МЭДЭН: good→the best, bad→the worst, far→the furthest.
commonMistakes: ❌ He is most tall.  ✅ He is THE MOST TALL. → Actually: THE TALLEST!
❌ She is the more beautiful.  ✅ She is THE MOST BEAUTIFUL.
❌ This is best restaurant.  ✅ This is THE BEST restaurant.
❌ It is most biggest.  ✅ It is THE BIGGEST.

page1.grammarTopic: Too / Enough — expressing excess and sufficiency
page1.grammarExplanation: TOO means MORE THAN NEEDED (negative meaning, there is a problem): too hot, too tired, too expensive. It comes BEFORE adjectives. ENOUGH means THE RIGHT AMOUNT (neutral or positive): big enough, fast enough. It comes AFTER adjectives. NOT ENOUGH means LESS THAN NEEDED: not big enough, not fast enough. Pattern: too + adj + to + verb → 'The soup is too hot TO EAT.'
page1.grammarExplanationMn: TOO нь ХЭРЭГЦЭЭНЭЭС ИХ (сөрөг утга, асуудал байгаа) гэсэн утгатай: too hot, too tired, too expensive. Тэмдэг нэрийн ӨМНӨ ирдэг. ENOUGH нь ЗОХИСТОЙ ХЭМЖЭЭг (саармаг эсвэл эерэг) илэрхийлдэг. Тэмдэг нэрийн ДАРАА ирдэг. NOT ENOUGH нь ХЭРЭГЦЭЭНД ХҮРЭХГҮЙ: not big enough. Хэлбэр: too + тэмдэг нэр + to + үйлүг.
commonMistakes: ❌ The soup is too much hot.  ✅ The soup is TOO hot.
❌ She is enough fast.  ✅ She is fast ENOUGH.
❌ There are too much cars.  ✅ There are TOO MANY cars.
❌ I am too much tired.  ✅ I am TOO tired.

page2.readingPassage: Bat wants to buy a new phone, but there is a problem. The phone is too expensive — he doesn't have enough money. He works part-time but his salary is not high enough yet. He says: "I am smart enough to find a solution and strong enough to keep trying. Next month I will have enough money — I just need to be patient!"

page1.grammarTopic: Would rather / Prefer to — expressing preferences
page1.grammarExplanation: WOULD RATHER expresses preference between two options: 'I would rather STAY home than GO out.' = I prefer staying home. After 'would rather': BARE INFINITIVE (no 'to'). PREFER TO expresses general preference: 'I prefer STAYING home to GOING out.' or 'I prefer tea TO coffee.' Structure: prefer + -ing + to + -ing / prefer + noun + to + noun.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: WOULD RATHER нь хоёр зүйлийн хооронд давуу байдлыг илэрхийлдэг: 'I would rather STAY home than GO out.' = Гэртээ байхыг илүүд үздэг. Would rather-ийн дараа: үйл үгийн ҮНДСЭН ХЭЛБЭР ('to' хэрэггүй). PREFER TO: ерөнхий давуу байдал. prefer + -ing + to + -ing. Prefer + нэр + to + нэр.
commonMistakes: ❌ I'd rather to stay home.  ✅ I'd rather STAY home. (no 'to' after rather!)
❌ I prefer coffee than tea.  ✅ I prefer coffee TO tea.
❌ Would you rather to go?  ✅ Would you rather GO?
❌ I prefer to walk than drive.  ✅ I prefer WALKING TO DRIVING.

page2.readingPassage: Bat and Sarnai have different preferences. Bat would rather go to a football match than watch a movie. He prefers outdoor activities to staying inside. Sarnai, on the other hand, would rather visit an art gallery than a sports stadium. She prefers painting to playing sport. But despite their different preferences, they get along perfectly. Good friendship is not about having the same interests — it is about trust, respect, and loyalty. They would both rather have each other as friends than anyone else in the world!

page1.grammarTopic: Adverbs — quickly, carefully, well, badly, hard
page1.grammarExplanation: Adverbs describe HOW an action is done. Most adverbs are formed by adding -LY to adjectives: quick→quickly, careful→carefully, slow→slowly. IRREGULAR adverbs: good→well, fast→fast (same!), hard→hard (same!), early→early (same!). Position: adverbs usually come AFTER the verb or AFTER the object: 'She speaks English WELL.' 'He drives CAREFULLY.' Never between verb and object!
page1.grammarExplanationMn: Тодотгол үгс нь үйлдлийг ЯАГАН хийснийг тайлбарладаг. Ихэнх тодотгол үгс тэмдэг нэрт -LY нэмж үүсдэг: quick→quickly, careful→carefully. ДУР МЭДЭН хэлбэр: good→well, fast→fast (өөрчлөгдөхгүй!), hard→hard. Байрлал: тодотгол үг ихэвчлэн үйл үгийн ДАРАА эсвэл объектын ДАРАА ирдэг.
commonMistakes: ❌ She speaks English good.  ✅ She speaks English WELL.
❌ He works hardly.  ✅ He works HARD. (hard is already an adverb)
❌ She quickly speaks.  ✅ She speaks QUICKLY. (after the verb)
❌ He drives careful.  ✅ He drives CAREFULLY.

page1.grammarTopic: I think / I believe / In my opinion — expressing and defending views
page1.grammarExplanation: To express opinions in English, use phrases like: 'I think...', 'I believe...', 'In my opinion...', 'Personally...', 'I feel...'. To AGREE: 'I agree!', 'That's a good point.', 'Absolutely!', 'Exactly!'. To DISAGREE politely: 'I'm not sure about that.', 'I see your point, but...', 'Actually, I think...'. Always give a REASON after your opinion using 'because', 'for example', 'such as'.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: Англиар санал бодлоо илэрхийлэхдээ: 'I think...', 'I believe...', 'In my opinion...', 'Personally...' зэрэг хэллэг хэрэглэнэ. ЗӨВШӨӨРӨХДӨӨ: 'I agree!', 'That's a good point.', 'Absolutely!'. ЗӨВШӨӨРӨХГҮЙ ЗОХИСТОЙГООР: 'I'm not sure about that.', 'I see your point, but...'. Санал бодлынхоо дараа ШАЛТГААН заавал нэм — 'because', 'for example'.
commonMistakes: ❌ In my opinion I think that...  ✅ In my opinion... (don't say both at once!)
❌ I am agree with you.  ✅ I AGREE with you. (no 'am'!)
❌ I think that English are important.  ✅ I think English IS important.
❌ Personally I am not sure.  ✅ Personally, I'M not sure.

page1.grammarTopic: Reflexive pronouns — myself, yourself, himself, herself, ourselves, themselves
page1.grammarExplanation: Reflexive pronouns are used when the SUBJECT and OBJECT are the SAME person. 'I hurt MYSELF.' (I hurt me — same person). Also used for EMPHASIS: 'I made this MYSELF.' (nobody helped me, I did it alone — this stresses independence). Forms: I→myself, you→yourself, he→himself, she→herself, we→ourselves, they→themselves. Common phrases: by myself (alone), help yourself (take what you want), enjoy yourself (have a good time).
page1.grammarExplanationMn: Reflexive pronoun нь субьект ба объект ИЖИЛ хүн байх үед хэрэглэгддэг. 'I hurt MYSELF.' (Би өөртөө гэмтлээ). Мөн ОНЦОЛЖ хэлэхэд хэрэглэнэ: 'I made this MYSELF.' (хэн ч тусалсангүй). Хэлбэрүүд: I→myself, you→yourself, he→himself, she→herself, we→ourselves, they→themselves. Нийтлэг хэллэгүүд: by myself (ганцаараа), help yourself (авч ид), enjoy yourself (хөгжилдөх).
commonMistakes: ❌ She hurt her.  ✅ She hurt HERSELF. (same person!)
❌ I made it by me.  ✅ I made it by MYSELF.
❌ He fixed it hisself.  ✅ He fixed it HIMSELF.
❌ They enjoyed theirselves.  ✅ They enjoyed THEMSELVES.

page1.grammarTopic: Should / Shouldn't — giving advice and recommendations
page1.grammarExplanation: SHOULD expresses ADVICE, RECOMMENDATIONS, and what is the right thing to do. It is LESS STRONG than 'must' or 'have to'. Form: subject + SHOULD + BASE VERB (no 'to'!). Negative: SHOULDN'T + base verb. Question: SHOULD + subject + base verb? Common uses: giving health advice, making recommendations, saying what is the right thing to do. Should = 'it is a good idea to...' Shouldn't = 'it is a bad idea to...'
page1.grammarExplanationMn: SHOULD нь ЗӨВЛӨГӨӨ, САНАЛ, зөв зүйлийг илэрхийлдэг. 'Must' болон 'have to'-оос ХҮЧИН ЧАДАЛ НЬ БАГА. Хэлбэр: субьект + SHOULD + ҮНДСЭН ҮЙЛҮГ ('to' хэрэггүй!). Үгүйсгэл: SHOULDN'T + үндсэн үйлүг. Хэрэглэх тохиолдол: эрүүл мэндийн зөвлөгөө, санал болгох, зөв зүйлийг хэлэх. Should = 'хийх нь сайн санаа' · Shouldn't = 'хийхгүй байх нь сайн санаа'
commonMistakes: ❌ You should to exercise.  ✅ You should EXERCISE. (no 'to'!)
❌ You shouldn't to eat junk food.  ✅ You shouldn't EAT junk food.
❌ He should exercises.  ✅ He should EXERCISE. (base verb, no -s!)
❌ Should you eat more?  ✅ SHOULD you eat more?

He thinks: "I should start following this advice tomorrow — and I shouldn't delay any longer!"

page1.grammarTopic: Some / Any / A lot of — countable and uncountable quantities
page1.grammarExplanation: SOME is used in POSITIVE sentences and OFFERS/REQUESTS: 'I have SOME eggs.' 'Would you like SOME tea?' ANY is used in NEGATIVE sentences and QUESTIONS: 'I don't have ANY milk.' 'Do you have ANY eggs?' A LOT OF / LOTS OF works with BOTH countable and uncountable nouns: 'a lot of eggs / a lot of milk'. A FEW (countable) vs A LITTLE (uncountable): 'a few eggs' / 'a little milk'.
page1.grammarExplanationMn: SOME — ЭЕРЭГ өгүүлбэр болон санал/хүсэлтэд: 'I have SOME eggs.' 'Would you like SOME tea?' ANY — ҮГҮЙСГЭЛ ба АСУУЛТ: 'I don't have ANY milk.' 'Do you have ANY eggs?' A LOT OF / LOTS OF — хоёуланд нь: 'a lot of eggs / a lot of milk'. A FEW (тоолж болох) vs A LITTLE (тоолшгүй): 'a few eggs' / 'a little milk'.
commonMistakes: ❌ I have any eggs.  ✅ I have SOME eggs. (positive→some)
❌ Do you have some milk? (question)  ✅ Do you have ANY milk?
❌ I have a few milk.  ✅ I have A LITTLE milk. (uncountable!)
❌ Add a little eggs.  ✅ Add A FEW eggs. (countable!)

page2.readingPassage: Sarnai is learning to cook. She has some vegetables but she doesn't have any meat. She has a lot of rice and a little oil. She calls her mother: "Mum, do we have any garlic?" "Yes, there are a few cloves in the kitchen." Perfect! Sarnai reads the recipe carefully and starts cooking. She adds a little salt and some pepper. There is enough food for the whole family. Bat tastes it and says: "This is a lot better than I expected — it is absolutely delicious!"

keyPhrases.english: She has a lot of vegetables
There are a few eggs left

speakingQuestions: 1.  What food do you have at home right now? Use some / a lot of.
2.  Do you have any vegetables in your fridge?
3.  Do you know how to cook? What can you make?
4.  What ingredients do you need to make your favourite dish?
5.  Would you like some Mongolian food right now?
6.  Is there much junk food in your diet? Should you change that?

page3.listeningScript:
Teacher: Today we practice some and any in cooking situations. Bat, do you have any ingredients to make soup?
Bat: Yes! I have some meat, a lot of vegetables, and a little salt. But I don't have any garlic.
Teacher: Can you make it without garlic?
Bat: Yes, but it is not as tasty. I might add a few more spices instead.
Teacher: Sarnai, what do you need to make tsuivan?
Sarnai: You need some noodles, a lot of meat, some vegetables, and a little oil. That is the basic recipe.
Teacher: Do you have any of those ingredients at home right now?
Sarnai: I have some noodles and a few vegetables. But I don't have any meat today. Maybe tomorrow!

listeningQuestions.question: What does Bat have for soup?
options: meat, vegetables, salt — but no garlic

Does Sarnai have all the ingredients today?
options: some noodles and vegetables, no meat

listeningQuestions.question: When might Sarnai cook?
'Some' is used in...
'Any' is used in...

homework: Writing: Write what food you have at home using some, any, a lot of, a few, a little.
Vocabulary: Write a simple recipe for your favourite dish using today's cooking verbs.
Speaking: Ask a family member: 'Do you have any...?' about 5 different ingredients.
Review: some = positive · any = negative/question · a few = countable · a little = uncountable
